description: Churros are ridged fried dough pastries, often coated with sugar and sometimes cinnamon. They are commonly served as a snack or dessert and may be paired with chocolate sauce or dulce de leche.
preparation_steps: Prepare a thick dough or choux-style mixture.
Pipe the dough into hot oil using a star-shaped tip.
Drain and coat with sugar, often with cinnamon.
Serve warm with a dipping sauce if desired.

health_advice.benefits: Enjoyable treat that is usually eaten in small servings.
health_advice.risks: Typically fried and often coated in sugar.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Eat as an occasional dessert and avoid very large portions with multiple sweet dips.

dietary_notes.note: Many basic churro recipes are vegetarian and can be vegan, but gluten-free versions require alternative flour and cross-contact control.
